exec_summary: Site traffic (GA4, 2026-04-25 to 2026-05-01): growthbook.io had 11,617 sessions and 6,953 users (+5.7% week over week).

GrowthBook's organic search (GSC) grew modestly this week: clicks rose 4% to 3,777 and impressions jumped 35% to 144,274, though the CTR dipped from 3.4% to 2.6% as more broad-match impressions came in. The homepage accounts for 2,107 of those clicks (56% of total), so the overall number is heavily weighted by branded search -- underlying content performance is solid but more distributed. Five new blog posts went live this week, with 'What Is A/B Testing' leading at 35 clicks and 61,443 impressions at position 8.1, and 'A/B Testing in the Age of AI' pulling 28 clicks from 13,406 impressions -- strong early signals for both.

The single most important strategic finding this week is Optimizely's cluster of vertical-specific experimentation use case content targeting financial services, healthcare, and retail. All five top opportunities this week belong to Optimizely, covering industry-specific homepage, mobile, and offer-page experimentation scenarios. These pages score at the top of our opportunity list precisely because GrowthBook has no equivalent content -- and buyers in regulated or high-consideration verticals (fintech, health) are actively searching for proof that experimentation platforms understand their context. This is a direct gap that Optimizely is filling and GrowthBook is not.

No new competitor pages were detected in this week's sitemap scan -- this likely reflects a monitoring gap rather than an actual content pause from all 10 competitors. Similarly, YouTube and AEO data returned no tracked videos or AI Overview citations this cycle, which points to pipeline coverage gaps rather than real silence. These are data quality issues to flag for the monitoring setup, not competitive signals to act on.
content_recommendations: 1. Write a vertical-specific experimentation guide for financial services (homepage and offer-page testing) -- Optimizely owns this space with multiple dedicated pages and GrowthBook has zero coverage; buyers in fintech are high-intent and this is a direct gap to close now.

2. Write a vertical-specific experimentation guide for healthcare -- same Optimizely cluster, same gap; healthcare is a high-trust, compliance-sensitive vertical where an open-source alternative story is compelling and currently untold by GrowthBook.

3. Optimize 'What Is A/B Testing' (blog.growthbook.io) -- it's already generating 61,443 impressions at position 8.1 with only 35 clicks (0.06% CTR); 'what is a/b testing' also appears as a striking-distance query at position 17.28 with 444 impressions on the main site, suggesting a title, meta, or internal linking fix could unlock meaningful click volume.

4. Expand 'A/B Testing in the Age of AI' -- 13,406 impressions in its first week at position 7.7 is a strong debut; adding depth (LLM-based feature rollouts, AI model experimentation, SDK examples) and building internal links from related pages could push it into top-5 positions for a high-growth query set.

5. Refresh the /compare page, which dropped from 5 to 2 clicks this week -- comparison pages are high-intent and close to purchase; given the competitive pressure from Optimizely on vertical use cases, making sure comparison content is current and comprehensive is a quick defensive win.
